priorities.label: S97 P1 — owl-b stray-flame open issue: the veo owl-b loop has an intermittent fire/ember licking in at the side edges + bottom, leaking through the (full-width) shared spirit-mask.
priorities.objective: Remove the stray flame visible at owl-b's mask edges (S96 open issue) WITHOUT degrading the (lovely) owl+lantern loop or touching the other 3 spirits.
priorities.scope: Ship a tighter owl-b-SPECIFIC render mask (public/art/nonet/owl-b-mask.png — the shared mask's alpha linear-stretched so alpha<~80 -> 0, >~175 -> 255: the flame regions (measured alpha 19-57) crush to transparent, the owl body/wings (alpha 100-231) stay bright). Wire an optional per-Guardian `mask` field in sudokuOverlay.ts; owl-b uses owl-b-mask.png, the other 3 keep spirit-mask.png. The owl-b.webm VIDEO is UNCHANGED (no re-encode, no veo re-shoot — the loop is good). OUT: re-shooting via veo; tightening the shared mask (would affect kami/owl-a/moss-b); baked video darkening (rejected — it dimmed the whole owl).
priorities.testing: Masked-composite render verification DONE (ffmpeg: original owl-b.webm through owl-b-mask.png over dusk -> owl bright, flame gone). tsc 0; vitest green (render-only, no node-tested path); build + bundle guard (PNG is an off-bundle asset); LIVE preview: owl-b clean + other 3 spirits unchanged + 0 console errors.
priorities.check_method: S97 P1: owl-b stray-flame cropped via a tighter per-guardian matte. DIAGNOSIS (empirical): extracted owl-b.webm frames + composited through the EXACT shared spirit-mask via ffmpeg (alphaextract+alphamerge over dusk) — confirmed a warm fire/ember flares cyclically at the side edges + bottom and IS visible through the matte (the prior 'cropped' note was optimistic). Sampled the shared mask alpha profile: flame regions sit at alpha 19-57, the owl body/wings/lantern at 100-231, and the mask is full-width (alpha>30 reaches x=0..540) — feathered only top/bottom — so the lower/side flame leaks. REJECTED two approaches empirically: (a) re-process owl-b from raw with a baked tightened matte over black — killed the flame but DIMMED the whole owl (multiplied the body, not just edges); (b) veo re-shoot — gambles the (lovely) loop + costs $. SHIPPED: an owl-b-SPECIFIC tighter render matte (public/art/nonet/owl-b-mask.png = the shared mask's alpha linear-stretched via ffmpeg lutyuv y='clip((val-80)*255/95,0,255)': flame alpha 19-57 -> 0, owl alpha >175 -> 255, smooth feather 80-175) + an optional per-Guardian `mask` field in sudokuOverlay.ts (owl-b -> owl-b-mask, the other 3 default to spirit-mask byte-identically). The owl-b.webm VIDEO is UNCHANGED. GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1488/1488; build+bundle guard green (entry FLAT 553.6/560 — owl-b-mask.png is an off-bundle public asset, sudokuOverlay chunk unchanged at 12.08kB); masked-composite render shows owl bright + lantern intact + flame gone; LIVE overlay introspection (dev import of SudokuOverlay + render(world) with sudoku active) confirms IN-CONTEXT wiring: owl-b.webm is masked by owl-b-mask.png, kami/owl-a/moss-b by spirit-mask.png, 0 failed requests, 0 console errors. Committed 77b7dbd, pushed.

priorities.label: S97 P2 — G3b Combo Codex: title-screen combo encyclopedia (Magic-14), discovered combos reveal (name+recipe+silhouette), undiscovered render as locked silhouettes; discoveries persist across matches.
priorities.objective: Close the discovery loop (BACKLOG Tier-1 G3b): a title-screen Codex that marks discovered combos and renders undiscovered ones as silhouettes. Today only a GODLY codex exists (codexOverlay.ts) and combo discovery is PER-MATCH (world.discoveredCombos is cleared on START_GAME / RETURN_TO_TITLE) — so this needs both a combo codex AND cross-match persistence.
priorities.scope: (1) New lazy comboCodexStore.ts (localStorage 'spark:combos:discovered:v1' — load/merge/persist a Set<ComboKey>, mirroring codexStore.ts). (2) Merge world.discoveredCombos -> the store at the render boundary (host AND client mirror both persist their own view) so it survives the per-match clear + abrupt quit. (3) New lazy comboCodexOverlay.ts: a grid of the Magic-14 (MAGIC_COMBO_KEYS) — each tile draws the combo silhouette via the existing bondVisual silhouette helpers (drawWheel/drawStar/drawAnchor/...); DISCOVERED -> full-color silhouette + resultName + recipe (e.g. 'Triangle -> Circle'); UNDISCOVERED -> dim/desaturated silhouette + '???' (shape teased, recipe hidden = brother-surprise, the codexOverlay convention). (4) Title-screen 'COMBOS' access button (titleScreen.ts) + main.ts lazy wire. (5) Tests: store load/merge/persist; tile discovered-vs-locked state; Magic-14 completeness. OUT: any combo SEMANTICS/balance change (that is the PARKED order-symmetry PDR); functional-placeholder combos (codex shows only the discoverable Magic-14); a protocol/schema bump (none — discoveredCombos already rides the snapshot since S88).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green + new comboCodexStore + tile-state tests; build + bundle guard (overlay is lazy-loaded -> off the main entry, like CodexOverlay); LIVE preview: open Combo Codex from title (undiscovered = locked silhouettes), play a match forming a magic combo, return to title -> that combo now REVEALED + persisted across reload; 0 console errors. Ultracode adversarial review workflow (correctness + persistence-determinism + 1v1-mirror + bundle) before commit.
priorities.check_method: S97 P2: G3b Combo Codex shipped. Built a title-screen Magic-14 gallery (the discovery loop was open — only a GODLY codex existed + in-match discovery was wiped on return-to-title). NEW comboCodexStore.ts (Pixi-free: localStorage 'spark:combos:discovered:v1', load/merge with VALID_KEYS filter + corrupt-data resilience, parseComboKey of the numeric SparkType enum, magicComboCatalog) + comboCodexOverlay.ts (lazy Pixi overlay: 14 tiles, each drawing the combo's real bond silhouette via drawBondVisual animated on Ticker.shared, discovered->type-coloured+name+recipe-glyphs / locked->dim '???'). Wired: shapes.ts SHAPE_GLYPHS export (additive), titleScreen 5th COMBOS button + onCombosSelected + combos in TitleButtonCenters/getButtonCenters, main.ts lazy openComboCodex + a render-loop persistence hook (lastDiscoveredComboSize rising-edge merge; host + 1v1 client mirror each persist), e2e helper key union. GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1495/1495 (+7 store tests); build green (entry 554.9/560, overlay its own lazy 3.4KiB chunk). LIVE-VERIFIED via the real path: COMBOS button -> openComboCodex builds 14 tiles (0/14 locked), merging discoveries reveals the correct names (Filament/Wheel) + count, discoveries SURVIVE a full page reload (localStorage), the REAL render-loop hook persists on world.discoveredCombos growth (ticker-pumped: ['0->1','2->4']->+['2->2']), glyphs tint to spec-IV SPARK_COLORS, clean boot (the only console errors were stale mid-edit HMR artifacts). 5-dimension adversarial review workflow (correctness/persistence/netcode/pixi-leak/regression) each independently verified -> 0 confirmed findings. Hardened: Ticker double-add guard + removed a dead var. Committed e1e7b6b, pushed.

priorities.label: S97 P3 — NONET spirit re-placement: the 4 guardians stand in symmetric, unnatural 2x2-corner spots; move them to organic, asymmetric positions (user playtest).
priorities.objective: User playtest: the NONET spirits/owls 'are standing in very symmetrical and unnatural places'. Re-place them so the framing reads natural (e.g. top-right, bottom-right, middle-left, bottom-left — varied heights/sizes, not a mirror 2x2).
priorities.scope: Retune the GUARDIANS array x/y (+ maybe vw/scale) in sudokuOverlay.ts only. Keep them clear of the centered NONET panel/frame (bounds [630,1290] x [210,1060]). OUT: changing the spirit art/videos/masks themselves; owl-b keeps its tighter mask.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green; build (no entry change); LIVE preview overlay introspection — 4 guardians at the new asymmetric positions, none overlapping the panel bbox, all still video+mask.
priorities.check_method: S97 P3: NONET spirits re-placed asymmetrically. Retuned the GUARDIANS array (sudokuOverlay.ts) ONLY — kami(elder) MIDDLE-LEFT (300,558) prominent vw360, owl-a TOP-RIGHT (1628,332) vw286, owl-b BOTTOM-LEFT (372,888) vw214, moss-b BOTTOM-RIGHT (1562,842) vw252 — varied heights+sizes so left(mid+low) and right(high+low) don't mirror. LIVE-VERIFIED via dev-import + render(world) with sudoku active: the 4 masked video sprites sit at exactly the new coords and 0 overlap the centred panel/frame bbox [630,1290]x[210,1060] (right spirits left-edge >1290, left spirits right-edge <630). tsc 0; vitest 1495/1495; bundle flat 554.9/560. (Visual 'naturalness' is the user's call on the next deploy; screenshots time out on the background WebGL canvas, so positions were introspected instead.) Committed 4b9447d, pushed.

priorities.label: S97 P4 — NONET winner jackpot celebration: solver gets fireworks + screen-wide victory lights + cheers/sounds + a 'you won' banner; losers see nothing new.
priorities.objective: On solve, the WINNER's NONET screen erupts in a jackpot celebration (fireworks + whole-screen victory lights + cheers/sounds + a victory banner). LOSERS see nothing new (the existing neutral resolve only).
priorities.scope: In sudokuOverlay render resolve rising-edge (line ~498, already has flood+sting+playNonetSolve/Lose/Timeout): add a WINNER-ONLY (ev.solvedBy === world.localPlayerId) fireworks/confetti burst layer + a jackpot light pulse + a new cheer/jackpot SFX (extend nonetJuice.ts) + a 'YOU WON' banner. Reuse the firefly/effect Graphics patterns; deterministic (keyed off world.tick - resolvedTick, the rainbowSwitchTick pattern). Gate strictly so non-winners get NO new celebration. OUT: changing the solve mechanic/scoring (winner x2 / losers halved stays).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green (+ a pure pose/celebration-window helper test); build + bundle guard; LIVE preview: winner path shows fireworks+lights+banner+cheer, loser path shows nothing new, 0 console errors.
priorities.check_method: S97 P4: NONET winner jackpot celebration. On a LOCAL win (ev.solvedBy === world.localPlayerId) the resolve edge fires startCelebration: playNonetJackpot (triumphant major run + bell sparkles + low swell, added to nonetJuice.ts) + 8 staggered fireworks + a full-screen gold glow (gentle ~1Hz breathe, capped 0.24 < the 0.30 charter, no strobe) + a popping 'JACKPOT!' banner. Animation math extracted to a PURE, unit-tested nonetCelebration.ts (hslToHex, makeFireworks, fireworkParticles, bannerPose, jackpotGlowAlpha/Color); rendered in sudokuOverlay via a celebrateLayer kept topmost. LOSERS/timeout: startCelebration is NEVER reached -> nothing new (only the pre-existing neutral flood). Render-only + per-peer-local (each peer compares its own localPlayerId to the host-synced solvedBy) => NO reducer/wire/protocol change, no desync. Cleared on new-trial (seed change) + dismiss (sudoku null). GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1502/1502 (+7 nonetCelebration tests); build green (entry FLAT 554.9/560 — all in the lazy NONET chunk). LIVE-VERIFIED via dev-import + a forced resolve: WINNER (solvedBy=me) -> celebrating true, layer visible, 8 fireworks drawn (fx bounds 212px), full-screen glow (1920px), banner alpha 1 scale 1.05; LOSER (solvedBy=other) -> celebrating false, layer hidden, 0 fireworks/fx; 0 new console errors. Committed bbca3d9, pushed.

priorities.label: S97 P5 — multiple godlies per match (1 of each type): after NONET, Voltkin would not trigger; allow each godly TYPE to fire once per match (1 Voltkin, 1 NONET, ...).
priorities.objective: User: formed NONET, then the correct Voltkin combo would NOT activate ('confined to one godly combo per game?'). Allow as MANY godlies per match as possible but only ONE of EACH type.
priorities.scope: INVESTIGATE the real cross-block first (RALPH:HUNT if needed): NONET uses world.sudokuFiredThisMatch (once/match); godlies use a per-PLAYER 60s cooldown (godlyCooldown) + activeCinematicPlayerId serialization — per the S97 Explore there is NO global godly-once lock, so the root cause of 'Voltkin won't fire after NONET' must be confirmed (sudoku-active gating? cinematic stuck? cooldown? effects-scan timing). Then implement the END STATE: a per-TYPE (per GodlyId + NONET) once-per-match guard so each type fires exactly once + nothing cross-blocks (worldTypes godlyFiredThisMatch Map, reset on START_GAME/RETURN_TO_TITLE, gate in findGodlyMatch, set in applyGodlyTrigger). OUT: same godly twice; godly power rebalance. NOTE: this touches synced reducer/world state -> protocol/schema additive-only, host-authoritative, 1v1-mirror safe; needs careful determinism review + likely a test.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green + a per-type-gating test (NONET fired does NOT block Voltkin; same type can't refire); build; LIVE preview: trigger two different godlies in one match; confirm 1v1-mirror/no-desync (reducer-only, host-authoritative).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #tighter-render-mask-beats-baked-darken-or-reshoot-for-a-stray-matte-edge-artifact: a localized generative artifact (a cyclic flame veo baked into the owl-b loop) was leaking through the shared full-width spirit-mask. THREE fix routes considered + two rejected EMPIRICALLY: baking a tightened matte into the video dimmed the whole subject (it multiplies the body, not just the edges); a veo re-shoot gambles a good loop + costs $. The winner was a per-asset TIGHTER RENDER MASK (crop) — the subject stays full-brightness inside the matte, only the offending edge band is cropped, the source video is untouched, $0, deterministic, and scoped to the one asset via an optional per-entry mask field (others default byte-identically). LESSONS: (a) to size the crop, MEASURE — sample the mask alpha where the artifact sits (flame 19-57) vs the subject (100-231) and stretch the alpha to zero the gap (lutyuv clip); (b) verify a matte fix with an ffmpeg masked-composite (alphaextract+alphamerge over the real bg) — it is PIXEL-EQUIVALENT to Pixi's alpha mask and far more precise + reliable than a heavy WebGL screenshot (preview_screenshot timed out on the 1920x1080 canvas); (c) prove the wiring IN-CONTEXT without a screenshot by dev-importing the overlay, render()-ing it, and introspecting which mask texture each video sprite carries (owl-b.webm <- owl-b-mask.png, others <- spirit-mask.png).

P4 #winner-only-celebration-is-naturally-netcode-safe-when-gated-on-local-vs-synced: a per-player celebration (winner jackpot, losers nothing) is trivially desync-proof when built render-only: the host writes ONE authoritative ev.solvedBy into the synced snapshot, and each peer's overlay compares it to its OWN localPlayerId — so the winner's client celebrates and everyone else's doesn't, with zero reducer/wire/protocol change. Extract the animation to a pure module (fireworks/banner/glow math) for unit tests; keep the photosensitivity charter (gentle ~1Hz full-screen glow capped < 0.30, put the flash energy in small-area fireworks not a full-screen strobe). Verify a winner-vs-loser gate by forcing ev.solvedBy to me / to another seat and asserting the celebration layer activates / stays hidden.

P3 #asymmetric-placement-is-data-not-code-verify-by-introspecting-coords: the 'spirits look symmetrical/unnatural' fix was a pure DATA change (the GUARDIANS x/y/vw array), not new code. Re-placed into varied heights+sizes (left mid+low, right high+low) so no two mirror, all kept clear of the centred panel bbox by keeping each spirit's x-RANGE outside [630,1290] (a bbox-x-interval test is enough — y is then irrelevant). Verified by reading the live masked-sprite positions + a 0-overlap check, since the WebGL screenshot tool times out on this background canvas. Visual taste stays the user's call on deploy.

P2 #verify-a-pixi-feature-without-a-screenshot-and-mind-background-rAF-throttling: G3b (a title-screen Combo Codex) was verified END-TO-END through the REAL UI path in the preview WITHOUT a working screenshot (preview_screenshot timed out on the 1920x1080 WebGL canvas). The technique: emit the real title button's 'pointertap' to fire openComboCodex, then INTROSPECT the live Pixi stage (find the overlay by a Text child, count tiles, read each tile's name/glyph-tint, read localStorage) to prove tile states + type-coloured glyphs + cross-reload persistence. KEY GOTCHA: the preview browser runs in the BACKGROUND, so requestAnimationFrame (Pixi's Ticker) is throttled/paused -> world.tick froze at 0 and the render-loop persistence hook never got frames, which looked like a hook bug. It was NOT — pumping app.ticker.update() once fired the real hook and persisted the discovery. LESSON: when a render-loop side-effect 'doesn't fire' in a headless/background preview, suspect rAF throttling and pump the ticker manually before concluding it's broken. Also: a 5-dimension adversarial review workflow (each finding independently verified) returned 0 confirmed findings, corroborating the live verification.
